differences.description: Document title changed from "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual: Volume II" to "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual, Volume II".
Version and release state changed from "Version 20250508: This document is in Ratified state." to "Version 20260120: Official Release".

Table of contents was reorganized: CSR listing now has subsections for unprivileged, supervisor-level, hypervisor/VS, machine-level, and indirect CSR mappings; later chapters were renumbered because several supervisor extensions were moved under Chapter 12 and Sstc became Chapter 13.
Added Section 12.1.12, "Supervisor Timer (stimecmp) Register", under Supervisor CSRs.
The standalone chapters for Svnapot, Svpbmt, Svinval, Svadu, Svvptc, and Ssqosid were moved into Chapter 12 as Sections 12.7 through 12.13.
Added Section 12.12, "Svrsw60t59b Extension for PTE Reserved-for-Software Bits 60-59, Version 1.0".
Added Section 15.2.19, "Virtual Supervisor Timer (vstimecmp) Register", under Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs.
Added Section 15.5.4, "Interaction with Pointer Masking", under Two-Stage Address Translation.

Pointer Masking no longer has a top-level "ISA Extensions" section with Ssnpm, Smnpm, Smmpm, interaction with SFENCE.VMA, interaction with two-stage address translation, and number of masked bits subsections; only "Number of Masked Bits" remains, moved to Section 18.2.8.
Added "Appendix A: Historical Rationale for Extensions" and subsection A.1 for Smepmp rationale.
Removed the standalone Bibliography entry from the table of contents; bibliography text is instead appended after Appendix A content in the second document.
Corrected the contributors sentence ending from "Reinoud Zandijk.." to "Reinoud Zandijk.".
Footer document title changed throughout from "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual: Volume II" to "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual, Volume II".
Added a new current-version preface for the 20260120 official release stating that the document contains listed ISA module versions, all of which have been ratified.
Added Svrsw60t59b Extension version 1.0 to the module list.
Added a change log entry since version 20250508: "Addition of the Svrsw60t59b extension for additional PTE reserved-for-software bits."
Renamed "Smctr" in the module list to "Smctr Extension".
Renamed "Svvptc" in the module list to "Svvptc Extension".
Renamed "Ssqosid" in the module list to "Ssqosid Extension".
Renamed "Pointer Masking" in the module list to "Pointer-Masking Extensions".
Added periods at the end of the Ssqosid and Pointer-Masking change-log bullets.
Changed the compatible-change bullet from "Specified synchronization requirements when changing the PBMTE fields in menvcfg and henvcfg" to include both "PBMTE and ADUE fields".
Added compatible-change bullet: "Defined the format of the memory-mapped msip registers."
Changed "Defined optional mseccfg and mseccfgh CSRs" to "Defined mseccfg and mseccfgh CSRs", removing the statement that they are optional.
Changed the cross-reference for Type-2 hypervisor execution from a chapter number reference to a named reference to "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support.
Changed "The separate debug specification proposal describes" to "The separate debug specification describes", removing "proposal".
Added that csr[9:8] pattern 10 represents hypervisor CSRs.
Changed the virtual-instruction exception cross-reference from Section 21.6.1 to the named section "Trap Cause Codes".
Changed the CSR-listing introduction from "Table 4-Table 8" to "Table 4-Table 7" because machine-level CSR tables were consolidated and indirect CSR mappings were added separately.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Supervisor Indirect CSRs: siselect at 0x150, sireg at 0x151, sireg2 at 0x152, sireg3 at 0x153, sireg4 at 0x155, sireg5 at 0x156, and sireg6 at 0x157.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Supervisor Timer Compare CSRs: stimecmp at 0x14D and stimecmph at 0x15D.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Supervisor Resource Management Configuration CSR srmcfg at 0x181.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Supervisor Control Transfer Records Configuration CSRs: sctrctl at 0x14E, sctrstatus at 0x14F, and sctrdepth at 0x15F.

differences.section: 2.2.3. Currently allocated RISC-V hypervisor and VS CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Virtual Supervisor Indirect CSRs: vsiselect at 0x250, vsireg at 0x251, vsireg2 at 0x252, vsireg3 at 0x253, vsireg4 at 0x255, vsireg5 at 0x256, and vsireg6 at 0x257.

differences.section: 2.2.3. Currently allocated RISC-V hypervisor and VS CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Virtual Supervisor Timer Compare CSRs: vstimecmp at 0x24D and vstimecmph at 0x25D.

differences.section: 2.2.3. Currently allocated RISC-V hypervisor and VS CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Virtual Supervisor Control Transfer Records Configuration CSR vsctrctl at 0x24E.

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Machine Indirect CSRs: miselect at 0x350, mireg at 0x351, mireg2 at 0x352, mireg3 at 0x353, mireg4 at 0x355, mireg5 at 0x356, and mireg6 at 0x357.

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added machine counter configuration CSRs: mcyclecfg at 0x321, minstretcfg at 0x322, mcyclecfgh at 0x721, and minstretcfgh at 0x722.

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Added Machine Control Transfer Records Control CSR mctrctl at 0x34E.

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Merged the Machine Non-Maskable Interrupt Handling and Machine Counter/Timer CSR entries from the former Table 8 into the single machine-level CSR table.

differences.section: 2.2.5. Currently allocated RISC-V indirect CSR (Smcsrind) mappings
differences.description: Added a new subsection and Table 8 listing currently allocated M-mode indirect CSR mappings for miselect values 0x30-0x3F, 0x70-0x7F, 0x80-0xBF, and 0xC0-0xFF, including iprio, eidelivery, eithreshold, eip, and eie mappings.

differences.section: 2.2.5. Currently allocated RISC-V indirect CSR (Smcsrind/Sscsrind) mappings
differences.description: Added Table 9 listing S-mode indirect CSR mappings, including AIA mappings, delegated counter mappings at siselect 0x40-0x5F, and CTR entry mappings at 0x200-0x2FF.

differences.section: 2.2.5. Currently allocated RISC-V indirect CSR (Smcsrind/Sscsrind) mappings
differences.description: Added Table 10 listing VS-mode indirect CSR mappings, including AIA mappings and CTR entry mappings at 0x200-0x2FF.
Moved the statement "All bits that are reserved for future use must return zero when read" out of the Table 10 caption and into standalone text before the misa Extensions table.
Changed commentary from "the repo around which development occurs" to "the project around which development occurs".
Changed the mhartid requirement from "at least one hart must have a hart ID of zero" to "one hart must have a hart ID of zero".

differences.section: 3.1.6.2. Double Trap Control in mstatus Register
differences.description: Added that whether performance counters and timers are updated in the critical-error state is UNSPECIFIED.

differences.section: 3.1.6.3. Base ISA Control in mstatus Register
differences.description: Changed commentary wording from "writeback" to "write-back" for HINT destination-register behavior.

Changed MPRV description from modifying the effective privilege mode for loads and stores to modifying it for explicit memory accesses; the MPRV=0 behavior likewise now refers to explicit memory accesses.

Changed WFI trapping text to say WFI may execute in "modes less privileged than M" and may raise exceptions in "modes less privileged than M" instead of "lower privilege modes" and "less-privileged modes".

Changed U-mode WFI rule: now executing WFI in U-mode when S-mode is implemented causes an illegal-instruction exception regardless of TW unless it completes within a bounded time; removed the statement that a future revision might allow S-mode to selectively permit WFI in U-mode when TW=0.

Changed MSIP description: instead of saying the bit is written by memory-mapped control registers used by remote harts and that a hart can write its own MSIP through the same register, it now says MSIP is written by accesses to memory-mapped control registers used to provide machine-level interprocessor interrupts.

Added specification of a hart's memory-mapped msip register as a 32-bit read/write register with bits 31:1 reading as zero and bit 0 containing MSIP, with changes reflected in mip.MSIP eventually but not necessarily immediately.

Changed STIP behavior to account for Sstc: if stimecmp is not implemented, STIP remains writable in mip for M-mode timer injection; if stimecmp is implemented, STIP is read-only and reflects the supervisor timer interrupt signal from stimecmp.
The RV32 counter access paragraph now contains the text "return bitj 31-0" instead of "return bits 31-0".

Added Sstc behavior for mcounteren.TM: when TM is clear, accesses to stimecmp or vstimecmp below M-mode raise illegal-instruction exceptions; when TM is set, access is permitted in S-mode and access to vstimecmp via stimecmp is permitted in VS-mode if not blocked by hcounteren.TM.
Added hardware-error exceptions to the list of exception types for which a nonzero mtval contains the faulting virtual address on instruction fetch, load, or store.
Added that on a breakpoint exception raised by EBREAK or C.EBREAK, mtval is written with either zero or the virtual address of the instruction.
Added commentary explaining that recording the EBREAK/C.EBREAK instruction address in mtval is redundant with mepc but provided for backwards compatibility.
Added hardware-error exceptions to the cases where a misaligned load/store or variable-length instruction fault may place the virtual address of the faulting portion in mtval.
Clarified the mconfigptr alignment formula: if MXLEN is 8×n, then mconfigptr[log2n-1:0] must be zero.

Added that after changing menvcfg.ADUE, executing SFENCE.VMA with rs1=x0 and rs2=x0 suffices to synchronize address-translation caches with respect to the altered interpretation of PTE A/D bits, with additional hypervisor requirements referenced.

Replaced the short statement that the Sstc extension furnishes STCE with a full definition of menvcfg.STCE, including that it enables stimecmp for S-mode, is read-only zero if Sstc is not implemented, blocks stimecmp access outside M-mode when zero, forces henvcfg.STCE read-only zero, and reverts STIP/VSTIP behavior as if Sstc is not implemented.

Replaced the short statement that Zicboz furnishes CBZE with a full definition: menvcfg.CBZE enables CBO.ZERO below M-mode when set; otherwise CBO.ZERO raises illegal-instruction exceptions below M-mode; CBZE is read-only zero if Zicboz is not implemented.

Replaced the short statement that Zicbom furnishes CBCFE with a full definition: menvcfg.CBCFE enables CBO.CLEAN and CBO.FLUSH below M-mode when set; otherwise they raise illegal-instruction exceptions; CBCFE is read-only zero if Zicbom is not implemented.

Replaced the short statement that Zicbom furnishes CBIE with a full definition of the menvcfg.CBIE WARL encodings: 00 causes CBO.INVAL to raise illegal-instruction exceptions below M-mode, 10 is reserved, 01 executes as flush, and 11 executes as invalidate unless configured by lower privilege to flush; CBIE is read-only zero if Zicbom is not implemented.
Replaced the short statement that Smnpm furnishes PMM with a full definition of menvcfg.PMM and a legal-values table for pointer masking; PMM is read-only zero if Smnpm is not implemented or on RV32.
Changed mseccfg from "an optional 64-bit read/write register" to a 64-bit read/write register that exists if any extension that adds a field to mseccfg is implemented; otherwise it is reserved.
Added detailed Zkr SSEED and USEED access-control rules for the seed CSR from U-mode, S/HS-mode, and VS/VU-mode.
Added Table 20, Entropy Source Access Control, specifying seed CSR accessibility and exception behavior by mode and SSEED/USEED settings.
Replaced the short statement that Smepmp furnishes RLB, MMWP, and MML with detailed normative definitions of mseccfg.RLB, mseccfg.MMWP, and mseccfg.MML.
Moved the Smepmp RLB rule into mseccfg: RLB allows locked PMP rules to be removed or modified when 1, and remains 0 until PMP reset if any locked PMP rule or entry exists while RLB is 0.
Moved the Smepmp MMWP rule into mseccfg: MMWP is a sticky WARL bit that changes the default M-mode PMP policy for unmatched memory regions from ignored to denied.

Moved the Smepmp MML rules into mseccfg, including reinterpretation of pmpcfg.L, M-mode-only and S/U-mode-only rules, Shared-Region encodings, restrictions on adding executable M-mode-only or locked Shared-Region rules, and execution restrictions for M-mode.

Replaced the short statement that Smmpm furnishes PMM with a full definition of mseccfg.PMM and a legal-values table for M-mode pointer masking; PMM is read-only zero if Smmpm is not implemented or on RV32.

Added commentary that Smmpm implementations need to satisfy max(largest supported virtual address size, largest supported supervisor physical address size) <= XLEN-PMLEN to avoid masking logic on the TLB access path.
Changed mseccfgh existence rule from "Register mseccfgh does not exist when XLEN=64" to "Register mseccfgh exists when XLEN=32 and mseccfg is implemented; it does not exist when XLEN=64."
Added that mseccfg.MML, mseccfg.MMWP, and mseccfg.RLB are reset to 0 unless the platform mandates different reset values.
Added that mseccfg.USEED and mseccfg.SSEED must have defined reset values and must not be undefined after reset.
Changed commentary wording from "mcause reset values may alias" to "To avoid ambiguity, mcause reset values may alias".
Changed AMOArithmetic definition from supporting "all RISC-V AMOs" to supporting "all RISC-V AMOs defined by the A extension".
Added Zacas AMOCASW, AMOCASD, and AMOCASQ PMA support levels and their meanings.
Added commentary that AMOCASW/D/Q require AMOArithmetic support because AMOCAS.W/D/Q require arithmetic comparison and swap capability.
Added that AMOs specified by the Zabha extension require the same level of support as corresponding instructions in the A standard extension or Zacas extension.
Extended the instructions covered by the misaligned atomicity granule PMA to include compressed encodings of the listed loads and stores.

PMP permission-fault text now includes cache-block management instructions as load-like operations requiring read permission and cache-block zero instructions as store-like operations requiring write permission.

Added commentary that address 2^(XLEN+2) cannot be represented as the top of a TOR range, so an RV32 system with 34-bit physical addresses cannot use TOR PMP to give less-privileged modes access to the uppermost word of memory.
Removed the statement that if the current XLEN is greater than MXLEN, PMP address registers are zero-extended from MXLEN to XLEN for address matching.
Clarified that when the L bit is set, R/W/X permissions are "additionally" enforced on M-mode accesses.
Reframed PMP matching and failure behavior in terms of individual "memory operations" rather than "accesses".

Added that misaligned loads, stores, and instruction fetches may be decomposed into multiple memory operations as described in RVWMO, and PMP checking is performed independently on each memory operation.

Removed the later paragraph separately describing decomposed misaligned loads, stores, instruction fetches, and stores wider than XLEN as possibly succeeding in part before PMP failure; the content is replaced by the new memory-operation wording at the start of the section.

Changed RV32 state-enable CSR description from saying the listed registers are 32-bit with corresponding high-half CSRs to saying there are CSR addresses for accessing the upper 32 bits of corresponding machine-level and hypervisor CSRs.
Changed SRMCFG field description to refer to the srmcfg CSR introduced by the Ssqosid extension, removing the old "Chapter 18" reference.
Changed introduction language: removed explicit SMAP and SMEP terminology and replaced it with a generic statement that two mechanisms combine to prevent the attack vector.

Changed RISC-V existing support description from "support for SMAP, through sstatus.SUM" and "SMEP by always denying execution... as mandated on the Privilege Spec" to support for the former through sstatus.SUM and the latter by denying supervisor execution of virtual memory pages marked with U.
The threat model is now Section 6.1 rather than Section 6.1.1; the former Section 6.1 "Introduction" heading was removed.
Added a sentence pointing to Appendix A.1 for the rationale that guided development of Smepmp.
Changed threat-model text from discussing lack of mechanisms in the "current (v1.11) Privileged Spec" to lack of the ability "Without the Smepmp extension".
Changed the description of attacks on Machine mode from those "mitigated through SMAP/SMEP" to those "mitigated through the virtual-memory system".
Renamed Section 6.2 from "Proposal" to "Smepmp Physical Memory Protection Rules".
Removed the detailed proposal text defining mseccfg, RLB, MMWP, and MML from Chapter 6; those normative definitions now appear in Section 3.1.19.
Added a short summary that Smepmp introduces the RLB, MMWP, and MML fields in mseccfg and points to the machine security configuration register for their detailed specification.
Renamed subsection "Truth table when mseccfg.MML is set" to introductory text for the physical memory protection rules when mseccfg.MML is set.
Removed the "6.2.2. Visual representation of the proposal" heading; the visual representation remains as part of Section 6.2.
Changed "all fields defined on mseccfg as part of this proposal" to "all fields defined in mseccfg as part of this extension".
Removed the Smepmp rationale from Chapter 6 as Section 6.4.
Added Appendix A.1 containing the historical Smepmp rationale formerly in Section 6.4, with wording adjusted to describe the ratified extension.
Removed the paragraph listing CSR numbers 0x321, 0x322, 0x721, and 0x722 for mcyclecfg, minstretcfg, mcyclecfgh, and minstretcfgh; these are now in the CSR listing.
Corrected mnepc masking text so the implicit read when IALIGN=32 is by the MNRET instruction rather than the MRET instruction.
Changed chapter title from "Smcdeleg Counter Delegation Extension" to "Smcdeleg/Ssccfg Counter Delegation Extensions".
Changed wording from singular "This extension provides" and "This extension also defines" to plural "These extensions provide" and "These extensions also defines".
Added that for a RISC-V hardware platform, Smcdeleg and Ssccfg must always be implemented in tandem.
Changed "When the Smcdeleg/Ssccfg extension is enabled" to "When the Smcdeleg/Ssccfg extensions are enabled".

Replaced the commentary note about hpmeventi, cyclecfg, and instretcfg representing subsets with normative paragraphs specifying that if Sscofpmf is implemented, event selector bit 62 MINH is read-only zero through sireg*, and if Smcntrpmf is implemented, counter configuration bit 62 MINH is read-only zero through sireg*.
Removed the later duplicate paragraph specifying the Sscofpmf and Smcntrpmf MINH read-only-zero subset behavior through sireg* because it was moved immediately after the table.
Changed the final sentence from "When the Supervisor Counter Delegation extension is enabled" to "When Supervisor Counter Delegation is enabled".
Updated commentary to state that mvip, mvien, and hvien registers are defined by the Smaia/Ssaia extensions; the first document mentioned only mvien and hvien.
Added that for a not-taken branch, ctrtarget holds the PC of the next sequential instruction following the branch.
Changed commentary wording from "Transfer Type Filtering bits" to "Transfer Type Filter bits".
Added hardware-error exceptions to the list of exception types for which a nonzero stval contains the faulting virtual address on instruction fetch, load, or store.
Added that on a breakpoint exception raised by EBREAK or C.EBREAK, stval is written with either zero or the virtual address of the instruction.
Added hardware-error exceptions to the cases where a misaligned load/store or variable-length instruction fault may place the virtual address of the faulting portion in stval.
Replaced short statements that Zicboz and Zicbom furnish CBZE, CBCFE, and CBIE with full U-mode execution-control definitions for CBO.ZERO, CBO.CLEAN, CBO.FLUSH, and CBO.INVAL.
Added that senvcfg.CBO controls only enable U-mode execution if the corresponding cache-block operation is enabled for S-mode.
Added senvcfg.CBIE behavior: if CBO.INVAL is enabled in S-mode to perform a flush, U-mode CBO.INVAL performs a flush even if CBIE=11; otherwise CBIE=01 performs flush and CBIE=11 performs invalidate.

Replaced the short statement that Ssnpm furnishes PMM with a full definition of senvcfg.PMM and a legal-values table for U/VU pointer masking; PMM is read-only zero if Ssnpm is not implemented or on RV32.
Added Supervisor Timer (stimecmp) Register section under Supervisor CSRs, containing the stimecmp/stimecmph definition formerly in the standalone Sstc chapter.
The stimecmp CSR description was moved from Section 19.1.1 in the Sstc chapter to Section 12.1.12 in Supervisor CSRs.

Clarified that if a leaf PTE is modified without a subsuming SFENCE.VMA, either the new translation or any older translation since the last subsuming SFENCE.VMA may be used, and subsequent accesses may use different translations from that set.
Added that the same unpredictable-but-well-defined translation choice applies even if the virtual-address width for that translation differs from the width currently specified by satp.MODE.
Added that global translations since the last subsuming SFENCE.VMA may be used regardless of ASID and virtual-address width.
Added that no SFENCE.VMA is required after enabling or disabling pointer masking because pointer masking applies only to the effective address and does not affect memory-management data structures.

PTE permission-fault text now includes cache-block management instructions as load-like operations requiring read permission and cache-block zero instructions as store-like operations requiring write permission.
Changed hardware A/D update rule from atomically performing "all tablewalk checks" to atomically performing "all page-table walk checks".
In step 9, clarified that the PMA/PMP check is for a store to the PTE at address a+va.vpn[i]×PTESIZE rather than an abstract store to pte.
In step 9, added that after setting pte.a and possibly pte.d, the implementation stores pte to the PTE at address a+va.vpn[i]×PTESIZE.
Sv48 leaf PTE page sizes are now explicitly stated as 4 KiB pages, 2 MiB megapages, 1 GiB gigapages, and 512 GiB terapages instead of simply pages, megapages, gigapages, and terapages.

Sv57 leaf PTE page sizes are now explicitly stated as 4 KiB pages, 2 MiB megapages, 1 GiB gigapages, 512 GiB terapages, and 256 TiB petapages instead of simply pages, megapages, gigapages, terapages, and petapages.
Svnapot was moved from standalone Chapter 13 to Section 12.7 under Supervisor-Level ISA.
Added that if the hypervisor extension is also implemented, Svnapot is also supported in G-stage translation.
Svpbmt was moved from standalone Chapter 14 to Section 12.8 under Supervisor-Level ISA.
Svinval was moved from standalone Chapter 15 to Section 12.9 under Supervisor-Level ISA.
Split the sentence describing illegal-instruction exceptions so that U-mode execution is separately stated before S-mode or HS-mode execution when mstatus.TVM=1; semantics are unchanged.
Svadu was moved from standalone Chapter 16 to Section 12.10 under Supervisor-Level ISA.
Svvptc was moved from standalone Chapter 17 to Section 12.11 under Supervisor-Level ISA.
Added new Svrsw60t59b extension specifying that PTE bits 60-59 are reserved for supervisor software and ignored by the implementation.
Added that if the Hypervisor extension is implemented, bits 60-59 of G-stage PTEs are reserved for supervisor software and ignored by the implementation.
Added dependency statement that Svrsw60t59b depends on Sv39.
Added commentary explaining operating-system use of reserved PTE bits for metadata and noting that Sv39/Sv39x4 otherwise require page-fault or guest-page-fault exceptions if bits 60-59 are nonzero.
Ssqosid was moved from standalone Chapter 18 to Section 12.13 under Supervisor-Level ISA.
Removed an em dash in the workload contention sentence, changing "controllers—the performance" to "controllers— the performance".
Sstc was renumbered from Chapter 19 to Chapter 13.

The Sstc chapter was reduced to an overview that points to the integrated stimecmp, vstimecmp, menvcfg.STCE, and henvcfg.STCE definitions, instead of containing detailed Machine/Supervisor/Hypervisor additions.
Removed detailed stimecmp definition from the Sstc chapter; it now appears in Section 12.1.12.
Removed the Sstc-specific STIP/STIE modification subsection from the Sstc chapter; the content is integrated into Section 3.1.9.
Removed the Sstc-specific sip/sie modification subsection from the Sstc chapter; the content is integrated into Section 12.1.3.
Removed the Sstc-specific mcounteren.TM subsection from the Sstc chapter; the content is integrated into Section 3.1.11.
Removed detailed vstimecmp definition from the Sstc chapter; it now appears in Section 15.2.19.

differences.section: 19.2.2. Hypervisor Interrupt (hvip, hip, and hie) Registers
differences.description: Removed the Sstc-specific hip/hie VSTIP/VSTIE modification subsection from the Sstc chapter; the content is integrated into Section 15.2.3.
Removed the Sstc-specific hcounteren.TM subsection from the Sstc chapter; the content is integrated into Section 15.2.6.
Removed standalone STCE environment-configuration subsection from the Sstc chapter; menvcfg.STCE and henvcfg.STCE are now specified in their respective CSR sections.
Sscofpmf was renumbered from Chapter 20 to Chapter 14.
Hypervisor extension was renumbered from Chapter 21 to Chapter 15.

Added HUPMM field description: when Ssnpm is implemented, hstatus.HUPMM controls pointer masking for HLV.* and HSV.* in U-mode when their explicit memory access is as VU-mode; HUPMM is read-only zero if Ssnpm is not implemented or on RV32.
Added commentary that the hypervisor should copy the guest's senvcfg.PMM value to hstatus.HUPMM before invoking HLV.* or HSV.* in U-mode.

differences.section: 15.2.3. Hypervisor Interrupt (hvip, hip, and hie) Registers
differences.description: Changed VSTIP description to include Sstc: VSTIP is the logical-OR of hvip.VSTIP and, when Sstc is implemented, the timer interrupt signal from vstimecmp.

differences.section: 15.2.3. Hypervisor Interrupt (hvip, hip, and hie) Registers
differences.description: Added that hip.VSTIP is set by writing vstimecmp with a value less than or equal to time+htimedelta and cleared by writing vstimecmp with a greater value, with the sum truncated to 64 bits.

Replaced the short statement that Sstc furnishes STCE with a full henvcfg.STCE definition: it enables vstimecmp for VS-mode, is read-only zero if Sstc is not implemented, and when zero causes VS access to stimecmp/vstimecmp to raise virtual-instruction exceptions and VSTIP to revert as if Sstc is not implemented.
Replaced the short statement that Zicboz furnishes CBZE with a full definition of henvcfg.CBZE controlling CBO.ZERO execution in VS/VU when HS-qualified.
Replaced the short statement that Zicbom furnishes CBCFE with a full definition of henvcfg.CBCFE controlling CBO.CLEAN and CBO.FLUSH execution in VS/VU when HS-qualified.

Replaced the short statement that Zicbom furnishes CBIE with a full definition of henvcfg.CBIE controlling CBO.INVAL execution in VS/VU when HS-qualified, including encodings 01 and 11 and reserved encoding 10.

Replaced the short statement that Ssnpm furnishes PMM with a full definition of henvcfg.PMM and a legal-values table for VS-mode pointer masking; PMM is read-only zero if Ssnpm is not implemented or on RV32.

Added Sstc behavior for hcounteren.TM: when TM is clear, VS-mode access to vstimecmp via stimecmp raises a virtual-instruction exception if mcounteren.TM is set; when both bits are set, VS-mode access to vstimecmp is permitted if implemented.
Added Virtual Supervisor Timer (vstimecmp) Register section under Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs, containing the vstimecmp/vstimecmph definition formerly in the standalone Sstc chapter.

differences.section: 15.2.18. Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (vsatp) Register
differences.description: Changed commentary from "misspeculatively executed" virtual-machine load/store instructions to "mispredicted" virtual-machine load/store instructions when describing VS-stage A-bit updates.

differences.section: 15.3.1. Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions
differences.description: Added that memory accesses performed by HLVX.* instructions are not subject to pointer masking.

differences.section: 15.3.1. Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions
differences.description: Added commentary explaining HLVX.* instructions are exempt from pointer masking because they emulate implicit instruction fetches, and pointer masking likewise does not apply when MXR is set.

Changed description of the G bit in G-stage PTEs from "reserved for future standard use" to "currently not used" while retaining the requirement that software clear it for forward compatibility and hardware ignore it.

Expanded synchronization requirements for changes to environment fields: if PBMTE or ADUE in menvcfg changes, HFENCE.GVMA synchronizes altered interpretation of G-stage and VS-stage PTEs' PBMT and A/D fields; if PBMTE or ADUE in henvcfg changes, HFENCE.VVMA synchronizes altered interpretation of VS-stage PTEs' PBMT and A/D fields.
Updated world-switch commentary to mention henvcfg.PBMTE/ADUE rather than only henvcfg.PBMTE.

Added section describing interaction between pointer masking and two-stage address translation, including GPAs being two bits wider, masking of GPA bits when vsatp.MODE=Bare, and duplicate TLB-entry implementation considerations.

Added requirement that hypervisors should execute HFENCE.GVMA with rs1=x0 when henvcfg.PMM changes to or from values where XLEN-PMLEN is less than the GPA width supported by hgatp.MODE, specifically PMLEN=7 with Sv57x4, PMLEN=16 with Sv57x4, and PMLEN=16 with Sv48x4.
Added that address-specific HFENCE.GVMA implementations should either ignore the address argument or ignore top masked GPA bits of entries when comparing address matches.
In the hypervisor mcause/scause table, interrupt exception code 13 changed from "Reserved for counter-overflow interrupt" to "Counter-overflow interrupt".

differences.section: 15.6.3. Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for mtinst or htinst
differences.description: Figure captions for transformed load and store instructions no longer say "noncompressed"; they now say "Transformed load instruction" and "Transformed store instruction".
Control-flow Integrity chapter was renumbered from Chapter 22 to Chapter 16.

differences.section: 16.1.2. Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps
differences.description: Removed the explicit parenthetical reference "See [LP_INST]" after describing a software-check exception caused by the LPAD instruction itself.

Changed Bare-mode shadow stack rule from prohibiting shadow stack memory accesses and causing shadow stack instructions to raise store/AMO access-fault exceptions to the narrower statement that shadow stack instructions raise store/AMO access-fault exceptions when effective privilege is below M.
Changed commentary wording from "access-fault" and "page fault" with hyphenated terms in one sentence to "access fault" and "page fault" without hyphens; semantics are unchanged.
Ssdbltrp was renumbered from Chapter 23 to Chapter 17.
Pointer Masking Extensions was renumbered from Chapter 24 to Chapter 18.
Changed parenthetical list from "U, S and M" to "U, S, and M".
Changed the cross-reference for extension optionality from Section 24.2.7 to Section 18.2.7.
Changed "page table walks" to "page-table walks" in the effective-address definition.

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: Removed C.LQSP and C.SQSP from the list of compressed instructions subject to pointer masking.

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: Changed the Hypervisor Extension cross-reference for HLV.* and HSV.* pointer masking from Section 24.3.1 to the Hypervisor Status (hstatus) Register section.

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: Qualified hardware CSR writes and debug address-trigger matching by adding "when applicable" to pointer masking application.

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: Changed the stval example to say hardware applies pointer masking to an address written into stval if pointer masking is applicable to that address, rather than unconditionally applying pointer masking to any address written into stval.
Added cross-references in the Ssnpm bullet to senvcfg, henvcfg, hstatus, and interaction with pointer masking.
Added cross-reference in the Smnpm bullet to Machine Environment Configuration (menvcfg) Register.
Added cross-reference in the Smmpm bullet to Machine Security Configuration (mseccfg) Register.
Removed the sentence "See Section 24.3 for details on how each of these extensions is configured" because the configuration details were moved to individual CSR sections.
Changed pointer-masking RV32/XLEN control text to refer to detailed CSR sections instead of Section 24.3.
Moved commentary about possible future additional CSRs for pointer masking from the former ISA Extensions section into Section 18.2.7.
Moved "Number of Masked Bits" from Section 24.3.6 under ISA Extensions to Section 18.2.8 under Background.

Removed the standalone ISA Extensions section describing Smmpm, Smnpm, and Ssnpm and the shared PMM WARL field; those definitions were moved to mseccfg, menvcfg, senvcfg, henvcfg, and hstatus sections.
Removed Table 55, "Possible values of PMM WARL field", from the Pointer Masking chapter; equivalent PMM legal-values tables now appear in the relevant CSR sections.
Removed Ssnpm subsection from the Pointer Masking chapter; its senvcfg.PMM, henvcfg.PMM, and hstatus.HUPMM definitions are now integrated into those CSR sections.
Removed Smnpm subsection from the Pointer Masking chapter; its menvcfg.PMM definition is now integrated into the menvcfg section.
Removed Smmpm subsection from the Pointer Masking chapter; its mseccfg.PMM definition is now integrated into the mseccfg section.
Removed standalone pointer-masking interaction-with-SFENCE.VMA subsection; the statement that no SFENCE.VMA is required after enabling/disabling pointer masking now appears in Section 12.2.1.
Removed standalone pointer-masking interaction-with-two-stage-address-translation subsection; the content now appears in Section 15.5.4.
Instruction set listings chapter was renumbered from Chapter 25 to Chapter 19.
Added "Control Transfer Records Management Instructions" category and SCTRCLR instruction encoding 0001000 00100 00000 000 00000 1110011.
History chapter was renumbered from Chapter 26 to Chapter 20.
Bibliography is no longer a standalone section/page; bibliography entries appear appended at the end of Appendix A.1 text in the second document.
